what_it_does: Documents the locked four-root axis tree for a code/design-substrate ledger, listing the root axes, how five prior naming schemes map to those roots, a separate connection-tuple, sub-type assignments, locked-vs-provisional status, formal backing, and build implications.
observations: Four roots locked by Tim on 2026-06-16: SCALE/HIERARCHY, TIME, FRAME/PERSPECTIVE, STATE/PHASE.
Hierarchy was folded into SCALE, making root-1 a single axis.
Provenance/derivation/influence/structural-semantic concerns were assigned to a separate CONNECTION-TUPLE (CAND-13), not a coordinate root.
Five naming schemes (fork, composition, wildcard, FRD vault, GLOSSARY) are mapped to roots, the connection-tuple, or sub-types.
Root-4 (STATE/PHASE) is backed by the FRD/unification-vault equation scale-phase-lock τ/n=(1/n)^k.
Sub-type catalogues and connection-tuple membership are explicitly marked provisional.
The axes are proposed as the front-interface coordinate system: zoom/rung, scrubber, whose-view, proposal↔collapsed state.
standouts: Tim-locked four-root count and names resolve prior contention.
Provenance/derivation/influence cluster is demoted from coordinate root to a connection-tuple.
The equation's scale-phase-lock is cited as formal ground for root-4.
Abstract axes are directly mapped to concrete UI navigation dimensions.
conventions: Uses a metadata block (trust, author, date, companion) at the top.
Labels content as LOCKED vs provisional-refinable.
Uses tables and code-like blocks to organize mappings.
Relies heavily on internal jargon and cross-references (CAND-13, Pi/κ, DNA, FRD vault, companion files).
concerns: Many key terms (CAND-13, Pi/κ, DNA identity, FRD vault, τ/n=(1/n)^k) are not defined inside this file.
Sub-type assignments and connection-tuple membership are explicitly provisional.
Build-pivot claims depend on companion files not included here.
Dense neologisms and shorthand may limit standalone readability.
notes: This file is a design-lock record: it fixes four coordinate axes and a separate connection-tuple, while leaving the sub-type layer open.
questions: What exactly is the CAND-13 connection-tuple and which members belong to it?
What do Pi, κ, DNA identity, and the equation τ/n=(1/n)^k mean in this context?
How will provisional sub-types be validated or refined against the formal ground?
How does this axis tree relate structurally to the listed companion files?
purpose_vs_actual: The file states it is a locked, provisional-refinable axis-tree skeleton and it does exactly that, plus maps prior namings and spells out build implications.
apparent_themes: Arbitration and provisional locking in conceptual design
Separating coordinate axes from relational connection tuples
Formal grounding of ontology in equations
Mapping abstract classification to user-interface coordinates
intention_signals: It tries to stabilize a long-running, multi-session design debate into a buildable four-axis coordinate system, deliberately keeping finer sub-type details unresolved for later refinement.
novelty: It treats provenance, derivation, and influence as a non-coordinate connection-tuple rather than a root axis, and proposes the abstract axis tree itself as the front-end coordinate system.
contribution: Provides a locked four-root naming convention and a mapping table that translates five prior naming schemes into a shared coordinate structure, with an explicit boundary between locked roots and provisional sub-types.
summary_for_embedding: AXIS_TREE.md is a locked design document that defines four coordinate roots (scale/hierarchy, time, frame/perspective, state/phase), maps five naming schemes to them, assigns provenance/derivation to a separate connection-tuple, and proposes the axes as the front-interface coordinate system.
intention_for_embedding: This file was meant to resolve and lock the root axes of a conceptual ontology so downstream build work could proceed while leaving sub-type details refinable.
